mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, which increases present values of future cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of low-rate observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8% reflects severe labor market contraction that depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8%, or the mean return conditional on this trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer prices and near-zero policy rates creates deeply negative real interest rates that force capital out of Treasury securities and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of CPIAUCSL above 200 and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 45% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries when policy rates are pinned near zero, sequentially rising retail sales levels confirm aggregate demand normalization and lift corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than half of the observations within this regime over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing the threshold of 95 indicates that manufacturing output has normalized from recession lows, confirming supply-side recovery that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 95.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 500 thousand units reflect severely depressed residential construction that signals weak construction employment and household formation, weighing on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 500.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income that supports consumer expenditures and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of 5-day forward returns does not positively correlate with monthly changes in PAYEMS over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of unemployment above 8% and policy rates below 0.5% signals a distressed environment where markets anticipate aggressive monetary accommodation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE > 8% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical episodes.
